aliases: Baked lasagna with bolognese and bechamel

english: Baked lasagna with bolognese and bechamel

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002572 covers lasagna as a food category; no specific code for Emilian-style gratinated lasagna with bolognese and bechamel exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers lasagne as a pasta dish category. No specific code for gratinated Emilian lasagna exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for lasagna or this dish variant. SNOMED CT does not provide granular food-dish codes at this level.

main_ingredients: bolognese sauce (ground beef, tomato, onion, carrot, celery, wine)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Lasagna Gratinata all'Emiliana is approximately 350 g, representing one generous rectangular portion of layered baked pasta.

nutrient_source.title: Lasagna with meat sauce — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299) and composite estimate for Emilian-style lasagna with bechamel and Parmesan
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for lasagna with meat sauce (NDB 21299) used as the primary anchor. Values adjusted upward for fat and protein to reflect the addition of bechamel sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan cheese characteristic of the Emilian preparation. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors. This is a composite estimate; exact restaurant recipe will vary.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Lasagna Gratinata all'Emiliana. GI of 47 is based on published GI values for lasagna and pasta-based mixed dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021). Pasta dishes with meat sauce and cheese typically fall in the GI range 40–55 due to protein and fat slowing gastric emptying. GL per serving calculated as (47 × 15.4 g carbohydrate per 100g × 350 g serving) / 100 = 25.3, rounded to 25.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~1330 mg per restaurant serving of 350 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or avoid this dish, as a single restaurant serving can provide approximately 58% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~25 per serving) despite a low GI of 47, due to the large carbohydrate load from pasta sheets in a restaurant-sized portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume this dish occasionally in controlled portions (approximately 150–200 g), as the low GI of 47 attenuates postprandial glucose response, but the total glycemic load of a full restaurant serving is substantial and should be accounted for in the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from beef bolognese, bechamel, mozzarella, and Parmesan (~3.6 g saturated fat per 100 g; ~12.6 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this dish due to its combined saturated fat and sodium load; if consumed, a half-portion with a side of non-starchy vegetables is advisable to reduce overall atherogenic and hypertensive risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lasagna Gratinata all'Emiliana is a classic baked pasta dish originating from the Emilia-Romagna region of northern Italy, widely regarded as the birthplace of authentic lasagna. The dish consists of multiple layers of flat egg pasta sheets (lasagne) alternated with a slow-cooked Bolognese meat sauce (ragù alla Bolognese) made from ground beef and pork, tomatoes, onion, carrot, celery, and red wine, interspersed with a rich béchamel (besciamella) sauce made from butter, flour, and whole milk. The assembled layers are topped with shredded mozzarella and freshly grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese, then baked in the oven until the top is golden and gratinated. The result is a hearty, deeply savoury dish with a creamy interior, a slightly crisp cheese crust, and complex umami flavour from the slow-cooked meat sauce. Nutritionally, a restaurant serving (~350 g) provides approximately 578 kcal, 32 g protein, 27 g fat, and 54 g carbohydrate. The dish is a significant source of calcium (from dairy), iron (from meat), and B vitamins. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low), but the glycemic load per full serving is moderate (~25), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is high (~1330 mg per serving), which is clinically relevant for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or dairy allergies. In Singapore, it is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in lasagna gratinata?
Italian baked pasta with bolognese and bechamel
Is lasagna high in saturated fat?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Lasagna Gratinata all'Emiliana was found in a primary regional source. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy lasagna with meat sauce (NDB 21299) with compositional adjustment for bechamel and Parmesan. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for pasta-based mixed dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the USDA anchor is a well-characterised composite lasagna entry and the adjustments are nutritionally defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category and is not reliably present in the composite ingredients at a measurable level. All other -1 fields (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) have been populated with estimated values from the USDA composite source where available; only vitamin_d_mcg remains -1 as it is not reported in the source database. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese cuisines do not apply. No Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi local names are required or provided. The dish is served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore - Boat Quay on field survey data at SGD 31.50. Restaurant-specific nutrient values may differ from the composite estimate used here.
